perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby agree to jointly prepare and file with the applicable regulatory authorities this Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D and any future amendments thereto reporting each of the undersigned's ownership of securities of the issuer named herein, and hereby affirm that such Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D is being filed on behalf of each of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned may, at their sole discretion, jointly prepare and file with the applicable regulatory authorities this Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D and any future amendments thereto reporting each of the undersigned's ownership of securities of the issuer named herein, and hereby affirm that such Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D is being filed on behalf of some of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the undersigned 'agree' to jointly prepare and file. The changed text replaces 'agree' with 'may, at their sole discretion,' making the joint filing optional. This introduces uncertainty. Furthermore, changing 'each of the undersigned' to 'some of the undersigned' now allows the agreement to be filed by a subset of the signatories, contradicting the initial implication that all parties would jointly file.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned may, at their sole discretion, jointly prepare and file with the applicable regulatory authorities this Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D and any future amendments thereto reporting each of the undersigned's ownership of securities of the issuer named herein, and hereby affirm that such Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D is being filed on behalf of some of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning her or it contained therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the other, except to the extent that she or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. Dated: March 27, 2020. /s/ Shelby J. Butterfield Shelby J. Butterfield BUTTERFIELD FAMILY TRUST By: /s/ Shelby J. Butterfield Shelby J. Butterfield Co-Trustee 1
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates joint preparation and filing, whereas the changed text makes it discretionary ('may, at their sole discretion') and allows for filing on behalf of only 'some of the undersigned.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a unified action and individual responsibility based on that action ('each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments'). By making the initial filing optional and not necessarily including all parties, the subsequent clause regarding individual responsibility becomes unclear and potentially unenforceable for those who opted out of the initial filing. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning her or it contained therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the other, except to the extent that she or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that responsibility for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning her or it contained therein, will be determined on a case-by-case basis by the regulatory authorities; furthermore, the signatories shall be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of information regarding others signatories.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly delineates responsibility for filing and accuracy. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating responsibility will be decided on a case-by-case basis. It contradicts the explicit assignment of responsibility in the original text, making it unclear who is accountable and removes any assignment of responsibility, and creates an in-text contradiction. By stating each shall be responsible for other signatories information also.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned may, at their sole discretion, jointly prepare and file with the applicable regulatory authorities this Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D and any future amendments thereto reporting each of the undersigned's ownership of securities of the issuer named herein, and hereby affirm that such Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D is being filed on behalf of some of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The undersigned acknowledge that responsibility for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning her or it contained therein, will be determined on a case-by-case basis by the regulatory authorities; furthermore, the signatories shall be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of information regarding others signatories. Dated: March 27, 2020. /s/ Shelby J. Butterfield Shelby J. Butterfield BUTTERFIELD FAMILY TRUST By: /s/ Shelby J. Butterfield Shelby J. Butterfield Co-Trustee 1
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text assigns clear responsibility for filing amendments and the accuracy of information. The modified text creates uncertainty by stating that responsibility will be decided on a case-by-case basis and also extends responsibility to information concerning other signatories, contradicting the initial assignment and creating an in-text contradiction within the provided contradicted text.
